category_title: Students with CHARGE syndrome exhibiting challenging behaviors (kicking, biting, scratching) to escape tasks or due to lack of structure
student_description: David is an older adolescent with CHARGE syndrome, profound hearing loss, and severe visual deficits who engages in aggressive behaviors (kicking, scratching) to escape difficult or unstructured tasks. He uniquely requires high-contrast visual supports, concrete predictability, and functional communication alternatives, as traditional negative consequences inadvertently reinforce his escape behaviors.

criteria.rationale: David needs to learn efficient, appropriate ways to communicate 'all done' or 'help' so he does not need to rely on kicking or scratching to escape tasks.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes explicitly teaching and reinforcing functional communication (e.g., signs or visual symbols) for requesting breaks or assistance.

criteria.rationale: Due to David's moderate-to-severe visual acuity deficits and restricted fields, materials must be adapted to be visible and uncluttered.
criteria.check: The long-term plan specifies adapting materials to feature high contrast, single items, and no visual clutter, presented within his intact visual field.

criteria.rationale: David requires strict routines and boundaries to prevent anxiety and challenging behaviors stemming from insufficient structure.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates concrete scheduling tools, such as a communication shelf or object/picture schedules, to establish a predictable daily routine.
